title: Sudoku
intro: Sudoku is a logic puzzle played on a square grid. The grid is divided into several smaller square sub-grids.
goal: In order to solve a sudoku board, every square must be filled in with a number from 1 to 9. Your job is figuring out which numbers go into the empty squares by looking at numbers that are already filled in.
rule_row: First, each row must include every number from 1 to 9, and each number must appear exactly once.
rule_row_example: The highlighted row has the numbers 3, 4, 2, 6, 5, and 1. It needs the numbers 7, 8, and 9 for the row to be complete.
rule_col: Second, each column must include every number from 1 to 9, and each number must appear exactly once.
rule_col_example: The highlighted column has the numbers 6, 8, 3, 4, and 1. It needs the numbers 2, 5, 7, and 9 for the column to be complete.
rule_box: Third, each 3x3 sub-grid must include every number from 1 to 9, and each number must appear exactly once.
rule_box_example: The highlighted box has the numbers 8, 1, 2, 6, and 5. It needs the numbers 3, 4, 7 and 9 for the box to be complete.
control_select: Click on a cell to select it.
control_input: Enter a number in the selected cell (dark blue) by pressing number keys 1 to 9 on your keyboard. Numbers you entered will appear in blue, and can be changed again later.

compact.intro: Fill every empty cell in the {{rows}}×{{rows}} grid with a digit 1–{{rows}} so that:
compact.rule_row: Each row contains each digit 1–{{rows}} exactly once.
compact.rule_col: Each column contains each digit 1–{{rows}} exactly once.
compact.rule_box: Each of the {{word}} {{sqrt}}×{{sqrt}} boxes contains each digit 1–{{rows}} exactly once.
